title: In conversation with DeepMind’s Demis Hassabis | WIRED
text:
Culture

Mar 5, 2021 11:39 AM

# In conversation with DeepMind’s Demis Hassabis

Demis Hassabis, CEO and co-founder of DeepMind, joins Greg Williams to discuss how AI can impact scientific research, building multidisciplinary teams – and how to combat algorithmic bias

Save this story

Save this story

Demis and Greg explore DeepMind's move into scientific research, using AI as a tool to help better understand the world around us and building multidisciplinary teams to work with computer scientists to tackle some of the intractable challenges in science.

Designed as an extension of WIRED’s long-running live conference portfolio, these punchy and engaging podcasts feature the same high calibre of speakers featured at a WIRED event.

_This article was originally published by WIRED UK_

Messages sent to the FBI by an investigator with the Minnesota Bureau of Criminal Apprehension went ignored for at least two days, per records obtained by WIRED.

Caroline Haskins

[](https://www.wired.com/story/heres-what-can-happen-when-the-us-bombs-irans-nuclear-sites/=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_eca69047-04d6-4a7e-81d5-92a580151350_roberta-similarity1)

[What Happens When a Nuclear Site Is Hit?](https://www.wired.com/story/heres-what-can-happen-when-the-us-bombs-irans-nuclear-sites/=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_eca69047-04d6-4a7e-81d5-92a580151350_roberta-similarity1)

As strikes continue on Iran’s nuclear facilities, the real danger isn’t the explosion, but what happens if critical safety systems fail—and how that risk could spread across the Gulf.

Jethu Abraham

[](https://www.wired.com/story/when-satellite-data-becomes-a-weapon/=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_eca69047-04d6-4a7e-81d5-92a580151350_roberta-similarity1)

As war reshapes the Gulf, the satellite infrastructure the world relies on to see conflict clearly is being delayed, spoofed, and privately controlled—and nobody is sure who is responsible.

Gia Chaudry

[](https://www.wired.com/story/the-strait-of-hormuz-reopens-but-global-shipping-will-take-months-to-recover/=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_eca69047-04d6-4a7e-81d5-92a580151350_roberta-similarity1)

[As the Strait of Hormuz Reopens, Global Shipping Will Take Months to Recover](https://www.wired.com/story/the-strait-of-hormuz-reopens-but-global-shipping-will-take-months-to-recover/=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_eca69047-04d6-4a7e-81d5-92a580151350_roberta-similarity1)

After weeks of disruption, a ceasefire is easing pressure on the Strait of Hormuz. But backlogs, infrastructure damage, and delayed supply mean the system won’t return to normal overnight.

Jumana Naim

[](https://www.wired.com/story/we-were-not-ready-for-this-lebanons-emergency-system-is-hanging-by-a-thread/=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_eca69047-04d6-4a7e-81d5-92a580151350_roberta-similarity1)

[‘We Were Not Ready for This’: Lebanon's Emergency System Is Hanging by a Thread](https://www.wired.com/story/we-were-not-ready-for-this-lebanons-emergency-system-is-hanging-by-a-thread/=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_eca69047-04d6-4a7e-81d5-92a580151350_roberta-similarity1)

In Lebanon, nearly 1 in 5 people has been displaced by Israeli attacks, leaving the government to manage a modern crisis without modern digital infrastructure.

Carla Sertin

[](https://www.wired.com/story/the-uk-launches-its-dollar675-million-sovereign-ai-fund/=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_eca69047-04d6-4a7e-81d5-92a580151350_roberta-similarity1)

[The UK Launches Its $675 Million Sovereign AI Fund](https://www.wired.com/story/the-uk-launches-its-dollar675-million-sovereign-ai-fund/=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_eca69047-04d6-4a7e-81d5-92a580151350_roberta-similarity1)

In a bid to minimize dependence on technology from other countries, the UK government is plowing resources into homegrown AI startups.

Joel Khalili

[](https://www.wired.com/story/with-one-million-displaced-lebanon-turns-to-digital-wallets-for-aid/=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_eca69047-04d6-4a7e-81d5-92a580151350_roberta-similarity1)

[With One Million Displaced, Lebanon Turns to Digital Wallets for Aid](https://www.wired.com/story/with-one-million-displaced-lebanon-turns-to-digital-wallets-for-aid/=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_eca69047-04d6-4a7e-81d5-92a580151350_roberta-similarity1)

Amid mass displacement and collapsing trust in institutions, digital wallets are becoming critical conduits for aid, connecting diaspora donors directly with communities on the ground.

Carla Sertin

[](https://www.wired.com/story/men-are-buying-hacking-tools-to-use-against-their-wives-and-friends/=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_eca69047-04d6-4a7e-81d5-92a580151350_roberta-similarity1)

[Men Are Buying Hacking Tools to Use Against Their Wives and Friends](https://www.wired.com/story/men-are-buying-hacking-tools-to-use-against-their-wives-and-friends/=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_eca69047-04d6-4a7e-81d5-92a580151350_roberta-similarity1)

In Telegram groups, men are sharing thousands of nonconsensual images of women and girls, buying spyware, and engaging in doxing and sexual abuse.

Matt Burgess

[](https://www.wired.com/story/a-single-strike-wont-shut-off-the-gulfs-desalination-system/=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_eca69047-04d6-4a7e-81d5-92a580151350_roberta-similarity1)

[A Single Strike Won’t Shut Off the Gulf’s Desalination System](https://www.wired.com/story/a-single-strike-wont-shut-off-the-gulfs-desalination-system/=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_eca69047-04d6-4a7e-81d5-92a580151350_roberta-similarity1)

The Gulf’s water system is built with layers of backup, but it relies on continuous operation to hold.

Dana Alomar

[](https://www.wired.com/story/deepfake-nudify-schools-global-crisis/=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_eca69047-04d6-4a7e-81d5-92a580151350_roberta-similarity1)

[The Deepfake Nudes Crisis in Schools Is Much Worse Than You Thought](https://www.wired.com/story/deepfake-nudify-schools-global-crisis/=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_eca69047-04d6-4a7e-81d5-92a580151350_roberta-similarity1)

An analysis by WIRED and Indicator found nearly 90 schools and 600 students around the world impacted by AI-generated deepfake nude images—and the problem shows no signs of going away.

Matt Burgess

[](https://www.wired.com/story/how-the-internet-broke-everyones-bullshit-detectors/=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_eca69047-04d6-4a7e-81d5-92a580151350_roberta-similarity1)

[How the Internet Broke Everyone’s Bullshit Detectors](https://www.wired.com/story/how-the-internet-broke-everyones-bullshit-detectors/=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_eca69047-04d6-4a7e-81d5-92a580151350_roberta-similarity1)

From AI-generated images to restricted satellite data, the systems used to verify what’s real online are struggling to keep up.

Gia Chaudry

[](https://www.wired.com/story/ice-is-paying-the-salaries-of-this-towns-entire-police-force/=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_eca69047-04d6-4a7e-81d5-92a580151350_roberta-similarity1)

[ICE Is Paying Salaries and More for This Town’s Entire Police Force](https://www.wired.com/story/ice-is-paying-the-salaries-of-this-towns-entire-police-force/=_wired-article-bottom-recirc_eca69047-04d6-4a7e-81d5-92a580151350_roberta-similarity1)

Under a Homeland Security program, police departments around the US are signing up to assist in immigration enforcement. The cops of Carroll, New Hampshire, are going all in—and they’re likely not alone.

Sammy Sussman

WIRED is obsessed with what comes next. Through rigorous investigations and game-changing reporting, we tell stories that don’t just reflect the moment—they help create it. When you look back in 10, 20, even 50 years, WIRED will be the publication that led the story of the present, mapped the people, products, and ideas defining it, and explained how those forces forged the future. WIRED: For Future Reference.
